items.reason: New core sampler tests cover config normalization, invalid rates, threshold sampling, distribution, same-trace events, fatal bypass, disabled fast path, and concurrent config/hot-path reads.
New config tests cover flag/proto updates, disabled proto semantics, previous-good retention, ppm boundaries, populate-to-proto, missing config handling, and request-only derivation.
New access sampling tests cover CLIENT/ACCESS/REQUEST_OUT behavior, request-sampled forced retention, zero/full/per-event access sampling, logSampled marker semantics, and access message layout.

New facade tests cover Object/Stream/RequestOut recorder APIs, deferred providers, sampled-out provider skipping, async/shared_ptr patterns, and key lifetime/formatting edge cases used by refactored call sites.
New integration tests exercise macro-level runtime logging, diagnostic supplement sampling, PLOG/VLOG pre-payload decisions, fatal/check bypass, and background log bypass.
Updated RPC metadata tests cover LOG_SAMPLE_UNDECIDED/REJECT/ADMIT behavior under disabled, full, zero, and local sampling decisions.
New system tests cover cross-process sampling consistency and worker-to-client LogSampleConfigPb delivery through register/heartbeat paths.
Updated performance tests add 4000 QPS and sampled-in/out fast-path scenarios for request logging, access sampling, sampler-only decisions, and skipped field construction.
Updated log message tests replace removed rate-limiter expectations with sampler behavior, including propagated reject with diagnostic_rate zero and stream-expression skipping.
Updated logging test reflects new logSampled marker semantics for sampled, rejected, disabled, and worker access-log cases.
